Turn 18 ‚ÄĒ the residual obstruction tower closes by induction I used only the three attachments from this turn. The standing problem is still the over-G isomorphism question for őďS,őďH, i.e. whether there is a continuous isomorphism ő¶:őďS‚ÜíőďH with ŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS. The problem statement defines F=P‚čäG, the relators rS,rH, and the two quotients.

The previous turn had reached the residual congruenceS(Y0,Y1,q)‚Č°X2 (modI22),and left the right next task: prove Cm=Lm uniformly rather than test degree 22 as another isolated case.

The result of this turn is:The¬†residual¬†Ef,¬†mod2,¬†s=1¬†Magnus¬†obstruction¬†tower¬†vanishes¬†to¬†all¬†orders.This is not yet the final written over-G isomorphism proof, but it removes the last obstruction tower that had survived the earlier sweeps.

Work in the same residual setup:S(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚ąí1Y1‚ąí2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,Y=1+B,with target X2=1+A2. The previous turns had already reduced the surviving problem to this residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece with section twist included.LetR(Y0,Y1,q):=S(Y0,Y1,q)X‚ąí2.If

R‚Č°1(modIn),then the first nonzero homogeneous term ő©n is primitive, hence lies in the free restricted Lie algebraL‚ąô‚äāF2‚ü®‚ü®A,B‚ü©‚ü©.

The degree-n linear correction map is‚ąān‚ąí1:Ln‚ąí1‚äēLn‚ąí1‚ü∂Ln,(U,V)‚üľ[A,U]+[B,V],where in characteristic 2,[U,V]=UV+VU.

The key restricted-Lie recursion isLn/im‚ąān‚ąí1‚ČÖ‚é© ‚é®‚éß0,Ln/2[2],n¬†odd,n¬†even.(1)This is exactly the recursion that Turn 17 identified as the likely induction mechanism.The meaning is simple: odd-degree primitive tails are always ordinary boundaries, while even-degree primitive tails have one possible extra piece, namely a restricted square of a primitive root of half the degree. This is why every real obstruction candidate after the early layers lived inQ2m:=L2m/im‚ąā2m‚ąí1‚ČÖLm[2].

The only notation adjustment needed is to define Cm slightly more structurally.

Let Cm‚äÜLm be the subspace of available root directions: a vector W‚ąąLm lies in Cm if the lower-plus-high correction torsor can introduce the root direction W, with all lower layers still continuable, so that at degree 2m it can move the square class byW[2]‚ąąLm[2]‚ČÖQ2m.

For m=9,10, this is exactly the Cm language used in Turns 15‚Äď17. Turn 16 proved C9=L9, clearing degrees 18 and 19.

Turn 17 proved C10=L10, clearing degrees 20 and 21.For m=5,6,7,8, the earlier finite sweeps supply the base block:Cm=Lm (5‚Č§m‚Č§10).(2)

Indeed:m=5:

Turn 7 showed that the degree-8 kernel freedom maps ontoL10/im‚ąā9‚ČÖL5[2].

So every L5 -square direction is available.m=6:

Turn 8 showed that the full affine kernel through degree 11, not merely the na√Įve ker‚ąā10, maps ontoL12/im‚ąā11‚ČÖL6[2].

So every L6 -square direction is available.m=7:

Turn 9 showedkerD‚Č§13‚Ü†L14/im‚ąā13‚ČÖL7[2],so every L7 -square direction is available.m=8:

Turn 11 found that the high torsor alone missed one L8[2]-direction at degree 16, but Turn 14 showed that the lower Y0‚Ü¶Y0Y4 base deformation toggles exactly that missing direction. Thus the combined lower-plus-high torsor hits all ofL16/im‚ąā15‚ČÖL8[2].

So every L8 -square direction is available.

Together with C9=L9 and C10=L10, this gives the base block (2).Now the induction is short.

AssumeCj=Lj for¬†all¬†5‚Č§j<m.

Take W‚ąąLm.

If m is odd, then by (1),Lm=im‚ąām‚ąí1.So

W=[A,U]+[B,V]for some U,V‚ąąLm‚ąí1. A degree-(m‚ąí1) correction on Y1 and q introduces exactly this primitive tail at degree m, and the subsequent higher tails are handled by the same already-established continuation mechanism. Hence

W‚ąąCm.ThusCm=Lm (m¬†odd).

If m is even, (1) givesLm/im‚ąām‚ąí1‚ČÖLm/2[2].

So modulo an ordinary boundary, every W‚ąąLm has the form

W‚Č°Z[2](modim‚ąām‚ąí1)for some Z‚ąąLm/2. Since m/2<m and m/2‚Č•5 in the induction range, the induction hypothesis gives

Z‚ąąCm/2.

By definition of Cm/2, the lower-plus-high torsor can supply the square transgressionZ[2].

The remaining boundary part lies in im‚ąām‚ąí1, hence is introduced by an ordinary degree-(m‚ąí1) correction. Therefore

W‚ąąCm.ThusCm=Lm (m¬†even).

By induction,Cm=Lmfor¬†every¬†m‚Č•5.(3)This is the uniform statement the previous turns were pointing toward. It explains why degree 22 is not a new computation: its root is L11, and 11 is odd, so L11 is already an ordinary boundary layer. It also explains why degree 24 is harmless: its root is L12, and L12 is handled by the induction using the already-established L6 base block.Now let n‚Č•22, and suppose the residual congruence has been solved through

R‚Č°1(modIn).

The first possible tail iső©n‚ąąLn.

If n is odd, thenLn=im‚ąān‚ąí1,so a degree-(n‚ąí1) correction kills ő©n. This is the same odd-layer mechanism already used repeatedly in the history.If n=2m is even, then modulo ordinary boundaries the only obstruction is[ő©2m]‚ąąQ2m:=L2m/im‚ąā2m‚ąí1‚ČÖLm[2].

By (3), Cm=Lm, so the transgressionTm:Cm‚ü∂Q2m,W‚üľW[2],is onto. In characteristic 2, it is linear modulo ordinary boundaries because(W+W‚Ä≤)[2]=W[2]+W‚Ä≤[2]+[W,W‚Ä≤],and the cross-bracket [W,W‚Ä≤] is ordinary Lie material, hence dies in Q2m. This is exactly the argument used for degrees 18 and 20.

Therefore one chooses W‚ąąCm whose square transgression cancels the square class of ő©2m. The remaining degree-2m tail lies in im‚ąā2m‚ąí1, so a degree-(2m‚ąí1) correction kills it.Thus every residual obstruction layer can be killed.Consequently, for every N there are residual section-twisted lifts satisfyingS(Y0,Y1,q)‚Č°X2 (modIN).Since the finite Magnus quotients are finite-dimensional over F2, the solution sets modulo IN form a nonempty inverse system. Passing to the inverse limit gives an exact residual solutionS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2 in the completed residual Magnus algebra.So the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower is now closed to all orders.

What this does and does not prove:It proves that the only obstruction tower found in Turns 1‚Äď17 collapses completely. The most obvious abelianized obstruction vanished in Turn 1; the remaining nonabelian obstruction was reduced to this residual Ef-piece; the prior finite sweep cleared through I22; and the induction above now clears every future degree.It still does not, by itself, constitute the final printed proof thatőďS‚ČÖGőďH.The remaining work is no longer an obstruction calculation. It is the formal lifting/gluing step: one must write the filtered Nielsen‚ÄďHensel argument showing that the residual solution lifts over the full completed Z2 [[G]]-Magnus tower, that the non-Ef idempotent pieces are solved by the unit-coefficient argument, and that the resulting continuous G-operator automorphism plus section cocycle carries the closed normal closure of rS to that of rH. The history already identified the non-Ef pieces as unit-controlled and the abelianized change of basis as invertible, but the final global morphism has not yet been written out as a formal theorem.

ESTABLISHED: The residual Ef,mod2,s=1 section-twisted Magnus obstruction tower vanishes to all orders. The uniform induction proves Cm=Lm for every m‚Č•5, so every even square quotient L2m/im‚ąā2m‚ąí1‚ČÖLm[2]is hit by the root transgression W‚Ü¶W[2], and every odd layer is an ordinary boundary.

GAP: This is still not quite the final over-G isomorphism proof. The remaining gap is to package the all-order residual solution into a full filtered Nielsen‚ÄďHensel lift over the complete Z2 [[G]]-operator Magnus tower, including the harmless idempotent pieces and the section cocycle, and then verify that the induced automorphism identifies the closed normal relator subgroups.

NEXT: Write the global lifting theorem: starting from the invertible H1 -matrix and the all-order residual solution, construct a compatible inverse system of G-operator Nielsen automorphisms modulo the full filtration, pass to the inverse limit, and check that the limit descends to an over-G isomorphism őďS‚ÜíőďH.[RESIDUAL-INDUCTION-CLOSED]
